FFmpeg third-party notice for ffmpegMagic
=======================================

This application uses command-line tools from the FFmpeg project.

  Project:  https://ffmpeg.org
  Legal:    https://www.ffmpeg.org/legal.html

License
-------

FFmpeg is licensed under the GNU Lesser General Public License (LGPL)
version 2.1 or later. Some optional components and pre-built Windows
binaries used for video encoding (including H.264 via libx264) may be
compiled with GPL-licensed parts. When those parts are used, the GPL
applies to the FFmpeg binaries in use.

You should read the license texts on the FFmpeg website and the LICENSE
file shipped alongside the bundled ffmpeg.exe and ffprobe.exe.

Trademark
---------

FFmpeg is a trademark of Fabrice Bellard, originator of the FFmpeg project.

Attribution (recommended wording)
-------------------------------

  This software uses libraries from the FFmpeg project, licensed under the
  LGPLv2.1+ (and may include GPL components depending on the build).
  FFmpeg source and build information: see vendor/ffmpeg/win64/SOURCE.txt
  in the development tree, or the application's install directory when
  distributed as a package.

Bundled binaries (Windows installer)
------------------------------------

Windows installers built by this project bundle FFmpeg binaries obtained
from the BtbN FFmpeg-Builds project (GPL shared build), used under the
terms of the FFmpeg and libx264 licenses. Corresponding license files are
included next to the executables.

Source code for FFmpeg is available from:

  https://ffmpeg.org/download.html
  https://github.com/FFmpeg/FFmpeg

Patents
-------

Use of FFmpeg may involve patented algorithms. See the FFmpeg legal FAQ
at https://www.ffmpeg.org/legal.html for further information.
